jokes.text: He didn't say anything about SkinnerInAShredder.com
jokes.setup: Principal lists banned websites to discourage anti-Skinner sites
jokes.punchline: Bart finds loophole with even more violent-sounding site
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart finding creative ways around authority. Good economy and character fit.

jokes.text: Upskirt Dreams
jokes.setup: Documentary filmmaker's respectable credits listed
jokes.punchline: Last title reveals inappropriate subject matter
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that relies on shock value rather than wit. Economy is good but feels cheap.

jokes.text: Well, they're not good enough to wipe my lens
jokes.setup: References respected documentarians Maysles brothers and Barbara Kopple
jokes.punchline: Arrogantly dismisses them with camera equipment metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - shows Desmond's pretentious nature through industry-specific putdown.

jokes.text: No. Because they pass the savings on to me, the consumer
jokes.setup: Asked if mad cow beef bothers him
jokes.punchline: Homer misses health concern, focuses on savings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely missing the point while sounding reasonable. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Look, I give people a meat-like burger and some kind of cola
jokes.setup: Defending his restaurant quality
jokes.punchline: Admits products are only vaguely resembling real food
jokes.explanation: Honest admission of food quality that's funnier for its matter-of-fact delivery.

jokes.text: Good as new
jokes.setup: Caught stapling together abandoned burgers
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully declares recycled food 'good as new'
jokes.explanation: Krusty's shameless attitude toward food safety creates the humor.

jokes.text: Stay with the dink
jokes.setup: Documentary filmmaker giving camera direction
jokes.punchline: Cruelly refers to student as 'dink' while filming
jokes.explanation: Shows documentarian's callousness while breaking fourth wall slightly.

jokes.text: Springfield's Machiavelli of the monkey bars
jokes.setup: Describing Bart's playground dominance
jokes.punchline: Compares elementary school politics to Renaissance political theory
jokes.explanation: Clever high-low juxtaposition with good alliteration. Very quotable phrase.

jokes.text: I chuck them at nerds, girls I like. Whatever
jokes.setup: Explaining his dirt-throwing targets
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of hitting girls he likes
jokes.explanation: Typical kid logic about showing affection through aggression.

jokes.text: Bart's glory has gone the way of England's masculinity
jokes.setup: Bart gets hit with mud and loses status
jokes.punchline: British filmmaker makes self-deprecating national stereotype joke
jokes.explanation: Self-aware British humor about national stereotypes. Well-crafted comparison.

jokes.text: When you leave home in the morning, you may be kissing your dolls goodbye for the last time
jokes.setup: Talking about dangers of being hall monitor
jokes.punchline: Admits to kissing dolls goodbye, undermining tough guy persona
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin - trying to sound tough while revealing childish behavior. Great misdirection.

jokes.text: My sash says 'Ultraman'
jokes.setup: Continuing to try to sound intimidating
jokes.punchline: Reveals hall monitor sash has anime character name
jokes.explanation: Continues Martin's nerdy character while deflating any authority he might have had.

jokes.text: I've just been listening to Bach while reading at a sixth-grade level
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to appear like a normal student
jokes.punchline: Her idea of normal is still incredibly advanced
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa joke - she can't help being exceptional even when trying to be ordinary.

jokes.text: I haven't seen such a natural pair since half sandwich and soup of the day
jokes.setup: Commenting on Lisa and documentarian getting along
jokes.punchline: Awkward comparison to cafeteria combo meal
jokes.explanation: Skinner's awkward attempt at being smooth with institutional food reference.

jokes.text: Remember, as far as he knows, we still teach Math
jokes.setup: Leaving Lisa alone with filmmaker
jokes.punchline: Admits they don't actually teach math anymore
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about educational decline delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Huh? What? Go, Aberdeen!
jokes.setup: Willie appears to be sleeping in class
jokes.punchline: Wakes up cheering for Scottish football team
jokes.explanation: Classic Willie - even his dreams are Scottish. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Well, I am young, gifted and yellow
jokes.setup: Describing herself as best student
jokes.punchline: References song 'Young, Gifted and Black' but substitutes her cartoon color
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference adapted for animated context. Very economical and quotable.

jokes.text: So you see yourself more as a buffet-style intellectual
jokes.setup: Lisa lists many diverse interests
jokes.punchline: Compares her broad interests to casual dining approach
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that's both descriptive and subtly critical.

jokes.text: Picking and nibbling until one day you're 38 and managing a Barnes & Noble
jokes.setup: Continuing buffet metaphor about Lisa's diverse interests
jokes.punchline: Predicts specific mundane retail management fate
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific career prediction that's both insulting and plausible.

jokes.text: Or you'll grow up, slog your way through Mount Holyoke and squeeze out babies
jokes.setup: Warning Lisa about lack of focus
jokes.punchline: Crude description of traditional women's path
jokes.explanation: Relies on crude phrasing more than actual insight. Character feels mean rather than witty.

jokes.text: Even that kid that wears diapers is more popular. And he ain't popular
jokes.setup: Explaining how uncool Bart has become
jokes.punchline: References unpopular diaper kid who's still cooler than Bart
jokes.explanation: Good structure with clarifying punchline that makes the insult even worse.

jokes.text: Even I beat you up that day. After you passed out
jokes.setup: Milhouse claiming to have beaten up Bart
jokes.punchline: Admits it was only after Bart was unconscious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse - even his moments of triumph are pathetic. Great character comedy.

jokes.text: You haven't touched your Dinnerables.
jokes.setup: Family dinner scene with packaged meal
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'dinner' and 'Lunchables'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective brand parody. Economical wordplay that immediately conveys the concept.

jokes.text: Pfft! Talking won't get you there
jokes.setup: Bart says he'll become the biggest drunk in town
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses words, implies action needed for alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer wisdom delivered with perfect economy. Shows his expertise in drinking.

jokes.text: And what's that? - I'm gonna die alone
jokes.setup: Claims keeping options open lets him do what he wants
jokes.punchline: His goal is dying alone
jokes.explanation: Dark punchline subverts expectation of positive life goal. Very economical delivery.

jokes.text: Don't do it, little girl. I spent 30 years brushing the teeth of dead monsters
jokes.setup: Lisa considering paleontology career
jokes.punchline: Worker describes dinosaur work as dental hygiene for dead monsters
jokes.explanation: Creative reframing of paleontology work as mundane dental care.

jokes.text: Ew.
jokes.setup: Lisa reads about minerals formed by heat and pressure
jokes.punchline: Single word disgust at geological description
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat. Works for pacing but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Now who will press my reset button? Will it be you? Or you?
jokes.setup: Previous 'you or you' pattern established
jokes.punchline: Same pattern applied to mundane technical request
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that deflates the inspirational tone with mundane request. Functional escalation.

jokes.text: Boing!
jokes.setup: Store accepts checks
jokes.punchline: Homer makes bouncing check sound effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect sound effect joke. Homer's awareness of his bad checks is endearing.

jokes.text: You may be a smart kid, but you don't know much about not hurting people's feelings
jokes.setup: Lisa correcting Homer about telescope vs kaleidoscope
jokes.punchline: Homer turns it into emotional intelligence criticism
jokes.explanation: Homer turning his ignorance into a parenting moment. Great character depth and humor.

jokes.text: Thanks a lot, surviving Beach Boys
jokes.setup: Beach Boys music ruins stargazing atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Specifies 'surviving' members as if others died for astronomy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about band mortality delivered by 8-year-old. Good cultural reference.

jokes.text: For three seconds there, you were legally the mayor
jokes.setup: Two workers get shocked by power lines
jokes.punchline: Claims brief electrocution equals mayoral succession
jokes.explanation: Absurd constitutional interpretation of electrical accident. Very original concept.

jokes.text: No one ever wrote a poem about sickly orange barf glow
jokes.setup: Complaining about light pollution ruining stargazing
jokes.punchline: Vivid, disgusting description of light pollution color
jokes.explanation: Poetic complaint with visceral imagery. Good contrast between poetry and gross description.

jokes.text: say, getting a date, which is difficult for the geeky people
jokes.setup: Comparing light pollution to other problems
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly admits his own dating difficulties
jokes.explanation: Frink's social awkwardness on display. Predictable but fits character.

jokes.text: They're like hermit crabs
jokes.setup: Explaining why he can't leave observatory
jokes.punchline: Compares astronomers to shell-stealing crustaceans
jokes.explanation: Creative animal comparison for academic competition. Good visual metaphor.

jokes.text: That's not a hood ornament. It's a pacifier you spray-painted silver
jokes.setup: Bart shows off stolen 'hood ornament'
jokes.punchline: Nelson reveals it's just a painted baby item
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Bart's attempted coolness. Nelson as voice of reason is funny reversal.

jokes.text: Ha, ha!
jokes.setup: Nelson starts his signature laugh
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's wrong and uses pitch pipe to correct it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about Nelson's signature laugh needing tuning. Self-referential perfection.

jokes.text: Does it make you feel superior to tear down people's dreams? - Yes. Does it make you feel smart to question people's motives? - Yes. - Well, all right then
jokes.setup: Lisa and Nelson challenge each other's behavior
jokes.punchline: Both admit to petty motivations and accept it
jokes.explanation: Great dialogue structure with mutual honesty leading to acceptance. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Argh! I'd be happy to scrimshaw your petition
jokes.setup: Asked to sign petition about light pollution
jokes.punchline: Offers to carve it in whale bone instead of signing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sea Captain response - turning modern activity into nautical craft.

jokes.text: or a faded southern belle who needs the forgiving cloak of night to seduce naïve young delivery boys with more pizza than common sense
jokes.setup: Listing types of people who want darkness
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific description of predatory older woman
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific scenario delivered in flowery political language. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Named after its discoverer, Professor Artimus Deadly, who was, ironically, killed in the shower of 1853
jokes.setup: Explaining Deadly meteor shower name origin
jokes.punchline: Discoverer killed by his own discovery
jokes.explanation: Good ironic backstory with historical detail. Name 'Deadly' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Naturally, we blamed it on the Irish. We hanged more than a few
jokes.setup: Recalling past meteor shower
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of ethnic scapegoating and lynching
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor about American xenophobia delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: It's darker than a French chick's armpit
jokes.setup: Commenting on how dark it is for crime
jokes.punchline: Crude comparison to European hygiene stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Crude stereotype humor that feels cheap. Economy is decent but relies on gross-out.

jokes.text: This is great. It's darker than a French chick's armpit.
jokes.setup: Commenting on how dark it is for vandalism
jokes.punchline: Crude comparison to stereotypical French hygiene
jokes.explanation: Typical crude teenage humor but fits character. Relies on stereotype but delivered with appropriate crudeness.

jokes.text: Buonasera, Fat Tony. I park your car the way Mama used to do
jokes.setup: Bart disguised as valet with fake mustache
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate Italian stereotype performance
jokes.explanation: Bart's elaborate disguise with over-the-top accent. Good caper setup.

jokes.text: Try the cheese pizza. It's greasy like, uh, you
jokes.setup: Milhouse also attempting Italian accent
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insults mob boss while breaking character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse - tries to help but makes things worse. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Hey, heh, we're all gonna be murdered someday
jokes.setup: Realizes he insulted dangerous mobster
jokes.punchline: Philosophical acceptance of inevitable death
jokes.explanation: Dark existential humor from 10-year-old Milhouse. Great unexpected wisdom.

jokes.text: You mean that 10-year-old with a mustache was a phony?
jokes.setup: Luigi points out they don't have valet parking
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony shocked that obvious fake was fake
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the obvious disguise. Fat Tony's genuine surprise at being fooled works well for character.

jokes.text: You know, Bart, I really like spending time with you
jokes.setup: About to commit felony together
jokes.punchline: Milhouse gets sentimental at inappropriate time
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's emotional neediness surfaces during crime. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Hey, you're not John Ritter. - And you ain't that gorilla from the zoo
jokes.setup: Two characters collide in darkness
jokes.punchline: Each mistakes other for completely random entities
jokes.explanation: Absurd mistaken identity exchange. Both references are hilariously random.

jokes.text: No one's ever won Moe's drink-a-gallon-of-gin challenge. And no one ever will.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining his drinking challenge
jokes.punchline: Confident that deadly challenge will remain unbeaten
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe characterization - proud of impossible/dangerous challenge. Implies previous contestants died.

jokes.text: What am I, Super Dad?
jokes.setup: Moe asks if Homer knows Lisa's position on lighting
jokes.punchline: Homer acts like basic parental knowledge is superhuman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - treating minimal parental awareness as extraordinary ability.

jokes.text: I'll bet somewhere there's a horse drinking coffee
jokes.setup: Commenting on how bright lights affect animals
jokes.punchline: Imagines specific absurd animal behavior change
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random observation about light pollution effects. Very quotable image.

jokes.text: Don't you think you're overreacting, talking gumball machine?
jokes.setup: Lisa complains about family's sleep deprivation
jokes.punchline: Homer hallucinates Lisa as gumball machine due to exhaustion
jokes.explanation: Good visual hallucination gag showing Homer's sleep-deprived state.

jokes.text: Aw, my baby's first words.
jokes.setup: Security system says 'Palm recognized. Access granted'
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks electronic voice is Maggie talking
jokes.explanation: Good character stupidity moment. Shows Homer's ability to misinterpret obvious situations.

jokes.text: Gandhi also said less talk, more rock
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions Gandhi and civil disobedience
jokes.punchline: Bart attributes rock music advice to peace leader
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart misunderstanding of historical figures. Very economical and quotable.

jokes.text: I'll catch them on my tongue for luck
jokes.setup: Glass shards falling from broken lights
jokes.punchline: Ralph thinks catching dangerous glass will bring good fortune
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph logic - confusing dangerous activity with superstition.

jokes.text: I can't read porno by candlelight! Who am I, Abe Lincoln?
jokes.setup: Complaining about power outage affecting activities
jokes.punchline: Compares porn reading to Lincoln's legendary study habits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of lowbrow activity with historical greatness. Perfect Comic Book Guy line.

jokes.text: Whoa, I don't need drugs to enjoy this. Just to enhance it
jokes.setup: Otto amazed by natural beauty of meteor shower
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he doesn't need drugs but will use them anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto moment - starts philosophical then reveals true stoner nature. Great misdirection.

jokes.text: I wish Jimmy the Snitch could see this. JIMMY: Well, just open the trunk.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony making sentimental comment about meteor shower
jokes.punchline: Jimmy responds from trunk where he's presumably captive
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy reveal - sentimental mob boss unaware he could easily fulfill his wish. Perfect structure and economy with shocking implication.

jokes.text: I wish Jimmy the Snitch could see this. - Well, just open the trunk
jokes.setup: Fat Tony sentimentally wishes someone could see meteor shower
jokes.punchline: Reveals person is dead in car trunk
jokes.explanation: Dark mob humor with perfect timing. Subverts sentimental moment with murder.

jokes.text: Oh, great glayvin in a Glad bag. That meteor contains carbon-based molecules.
jokes.setup: Frink excited about meteor discovery
jokes.punchline: Nonsense exclamation mixing his catchphrase with product placement
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink nonsense exclamation. 'Glayvin in a Glad bag' is perfectly random Frink language.

jokes.text: Oh, great glayvin in a Glad bag
jokes.setup: Frink excited about meteor containing carbon molecules
jokes.punchline: His nonsense exclamation references trash bags
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink gibberish with brand name incorporation. Character-specific but not brilliant.

jokes.text: I wish God were alive to see this
jokes.setup: Homer moved by beautiful meteor shower
jokes.punchline: Implies God is dead in casual way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer theology - casually profound and absurd. Excellent economy and very quotable.

jokes.text: I'm gonna live with Underground Grandma
jokes.setup: Asked where he'll be in seven years
jokes.punchline: References mysterious underground grandmother figure
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Ralph vision of his future. Creates intriguing nonsense mythology.

jokes.text: I'll be batting .352 in the majors.
jokes.setup: Documentary asking where students will be in seven years
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific batting average prediction
jokes.explanation: Good specificity in the batting average shows kid's detailed fantasy. Not groundbreaking but well-observed.
